topic: Traveling by different modes of transportation
content: Various transportation options include buses, trains, boats, planes, taxis, and cars. Travel can be either near or far from your current location. Sightseeing and socializing are part of the travel experience.
learning_objectives: Understand different modes of transportation
Recognize the distinction between near and far travel
Identify activities that enhance travel experiences

content: Classify vehicles based on their movement (land, air, water) and identify words with the vowel sound 'u'.
learning_objectives: Classify vehicles into land, air, and water categories
Identify vehicles that move on different surfaces
Recognize the vowel sound 'u' in words

topic: Concept Block 2: Daily Routines and Punctuality in School Life
content: The concept of daily routines and the importance of punctuality in school life.
learning_objectives: Understand daily routines as structured paths from home to school
Recognize punctuality as a valued trait ensuring timely arrival at school
Identify how a predictable and organized structure benefits a student's day

content: Listen to the words that your teacher will read out. Point to the object that represents the word.
learning_objectives: Listen attentively to teacher's instructions
Identify and point to corresponding objects
Prepare relevant classroom materials

topic: Categorizing Vehicles Based on Their Types
content: Vehicles can be classified into various categories such as land, air, and water vehicles. Each category has unique characteristics that define it. Understanding these categories helps us appreciate the diversity of transportation methods.
learning_objectives: Identify the three main categories of vehicles.
Name a vehicle that belongs to each category.
Explain why it is important to categorize vehicles.

topic: Understanding the Concept of a Town
content: A town is a geographical area where people live and work, characterized by streets, houses, rooms, and beds.
learning_objectives: Identify the common elements found in a town.
Explain how streets contribute to the organization of a town.
Describe the importance of houses in a town.

topic: Understanding Street Names and Cultural Diversity
content: Encircle words from given columns and listen to teacher to identify street names and cultural elements.
learning_objectives: Sensitize children to the diversity of languages and cultures in India.
Identify and encircle words related to street names, areas, towns, states, and countries.
Follow instructions to listen and respond to teacher's prompts.

topic: English Alphabet and Letter Object Associations
content: Each letter of the alphabet has a unique sound, objects in our environment can be associated with specific letters, practicing these associations helps improve reading and writing skills.
learning_objectives: Understand that each letter has a unique sound
Associate objects with specific letters
Practice matching letters with objects to enhance reading and writing

content: Clouds are formations in the sky that can appear in various shapes and colors.
learning_objectives: Clouds can look like animals or objects.
Clouds can be different colors.
Clouds move across the sky.

topic: Observing and Describing the Sky and Ground
content: Children learn to observe and describe the sky and ground using sight words.
learning_objectives: Observing the sky and ground helps children learn new words and concepts.
Describing what they see in full sentences aids in language development.
Regular practice with sight words and sentence structures improves communication skills.

content: Arrange words, join dots, and color pictures to understand organization and creativity.
learning_objectives: Understand alphabetical order
Organize information systematically
Enhance visual learning through coloring
Improve memory and understanding through repetition
Develop creativity through artistic expression

content: A little fly forgets his name and tries to remember it by asking different characters.
learning_objectives: Identify the fly's quest to remember his name.
Understand how the fly learns his name by asking different characters.
Recognize the importance of associating new information with familiar objects.

content: In this section, we explore the importance of identity and the sounds of nature.
learning_objectives: Understand the significance of knowing one's name for personal identity.
Recognize that nature is full of unique sounds made by different animals.
Appreciate that listening to nature can be a calming and educational experience.

content: Every object or creature has its own unique sound. Children can learn about the world by listening to the sounds around them. This activity helps children develop their listening skills and curiosity about nature.
learning_objectives: Understand that every object or creature has a unique sound.
Learn about the world by listening to sounds.
Develop listening skills and curiosity about nature.

content: A cat asks a cow for milk in exchange for grass and water.
learning_objectives: Understand the simple exchange in the story
Identify the words 'milk', 'grass', and 'water'
Practice writing a short story using the given words

content: The concept of beauty is subjective and varies from person to person. It is not determined by physical appearance alone.
learning_objectives: Understand that beauty is not just about physical appearance.
Recognize that good qualities and character are important aspects of beauty.
Realize that everyone is beautiful in their own unique way.

topic: Diversity of the Animal Kingdom and Importance of Friendship
content: This section explores the unique characteristics of different animals and birds, emphasizing the importance of friendship and the qualities that make a friend special.
learning_objectives: Recognize the wide range of unique features in animals and birds
Understand the significance of friendship and good qualities in friends
Appreciate the educational and enjoyable aspects of learning about the natural world

content: The concept being taught here is rhyming words, specifically focusing on words that rhyme with the given words in Column A. The words in Column B are names of birds.
learning_objectives: Understand what rhyming words are
Identify bird names that rhyme with given words
Practice rhyming through exercises and examples

content: In this story, we learn about the consequences of our actions and the importance of being mindful of our environment.
learning_objectives: Understand that every action has a reaction.
Recognize the impact of human behavior on nature.
Identify ways to be considerate of the environment and its inhabitants.

content: Proper waste disposal is important for maintaining cleanliness and environmental health. Waste should be thrown in designated dustbins to prevent littering. Personal responsibility in waste disposal contributes to community well-being.
learning_objectives: Understand the importance of proper waste disposal.
Identify the consequences of littering.
Recognize the role of personal responsibility in maintaining a clean environment.

content: There was a monkey sitting ______________ of the tree. The two girls had a ______________ and a bottle of juice. The monkey threw the ______________ into ______________.
learning_objectives: Describe the setting where the monkey is sitting.
Identify the items the two girls have.
Determine what the monkey threw and where it was thrown.

topic: Encouraging Children to Express Likes and Dislikes During Shopping Experiences
content: Encourage children to express their likes and dislikes during shopping experiences.
learning_objectives: Share personal preferences to understand likes and dislikes
Discuss likes and dislikes to improve communication skills
Learn about different products and make informed choices

content: Water and kindness are essential for creating a harmonious world. Just as little drops of water and grains of sand come together to form the mighty ocean and pleasant land, small acts of love and kindness spread smiles and happiness.
learning_objectives: Water is a vital resource for life, used for drinking, cleaning, and nourishing plants.
Kindness and acts of warmth are powerful forces that can transform individuals and communities.
Harmony in the world is achieved when we recognize the importance of both water and kindness.

content: Draw one thing you feel would help us make our earth a more beautiful place. Reprint 2026-27
learning_objectives: Explain the importance of preserving natural resources.
Discuss the role of individual actions in environmental conservation.
Describe the impact of pollution on ecosystems.

content: Children are learning about the textures of various kitchen items.
learning_objectives: Understand that texture is a property of a surface that can be felt by touch.
Identify grainy texture as a surface that feels rough and uneven.
Recognize that identifying textures helps us understand and describe the world around us.

content: # Chapter 2: Cultural Diversity in India ## Core Concept India is a country with a rich tapestry of cultures, languages, and traditions. Each region has its unique customs and practices that contribute to the diverse cultural landscape of the nation.
learning_objectives: Understand the concept of cultural diversity in India
Identify key regional identities in India
Recognize the importance of cultural preservation through art, dance, and music
Explain the significance of Garba dance and its association with Gujarat
Name a popular festival in Tamil Nadu and describe its significance
Discuss how regional languages contribute to India's cultural diversity
Research and write about the significance of the Mridang in Indian classical music
Create a poster showcasing traditional attires from various Indian states
Avoid assuming all Indians speak the same language
Acknowledge the importance of regional festivals in cultural identity
Understand that India's cultural diversity stems from its varied geography and history

topic: Cultural Diversity and Personal Experiences in India
content: I have seen the Taj Mahal. Have you seen any monument? My name is Nonglik. I come from Manipur. I speak Manipuri. What language do you speak? Mridang Bharti is my name. I come from Andhra Pradesh. I speak Telugu. During my summer holidays, I go to Kashmir and stay with my friends Salma and Suresh. Where do you go for your holidays? We are two sisters. We belong to Odisha. We speak Odia. We decorate our walls and floors. Do you have a sister or a brother? Mridang New words festival | puppet | travel | painting | neighbour | fingerprints | My name is Unni. I come from Kerala. I speak Malayalam. My favourite tree is the coconut tree. Which tree do you like?
learning_objectives: To understand the variety of languages spoken in different states of India
To recognize personal experiences as a means to learn about different cultures
To appreciate familial relationships and traditions across India

content: Encourage students to use polite language and discuss language skills, drawing activity, core concept, deep understanding, check understanding, practice, common mistakes, quick revision
learning_objectives: Encourage the use of polite language in various situations
Discuss language skills such as speaking multiple languages
Draw something they like the most in their home
Explain the importance of polite language in social interactions
Discuss the benefits of being bilingual
Understand the importance of cultural diversity and respect for others' languages
Role-play scenarios using polite language
Create dialogues between bilingual characters
Identify common mistakes related to language usage
Revise key points about polite language and bilingualism

content: It was a bright sunny day. My brother went to buy bread. He crossed the bridge to reach the shop.
learning_objectives: Identify the official language of India
Recognize the Devanagari script
Understand the concept of a language as a system of communication

content: I like to eat mangoes but I don't like to eat apples. I like milk. I don't like tea. My grandfather is old. He is strong. I wanted to go out. I was too tired.
learning_objectives: Understand sentence structure and contrasting ideas
Recognize the relationship between two sentences
Practice sentence formation with given examples

topic: The Charkha and Its Role in Indian Independence
content: The Charkha, or spinning wheel, is a traditional Indian tool used for spinning thread from fibers, popularized by Mahatma Gandhi as a symbol of self-reliance and independence.
learning_objectives: Understand the primary function of a Charkha
Recognize why Mahatma Gandhi promoted the Charkha
Identify how the Charkha contributes to local handicrafts

content: Vanmahotsav is a festival that emphasizes the conservation of trees.
learning_objectives: The primary focus of Vanmahotsav is the planting and nurturing of trees.
Trees benefit the environment by reducing air pollution, providing shade, preventing soil erosion, and supporting biodiversity.
Activities during Vanmahotsav include planting saplings, painting trees, and creating artwork.

topic: The Sun as a Source of Energy and Cultural Festivals
content: The sun is the primary source of energy for Earth, and various cultures have festivals that honor this celestial body.
learning_objectives: Identify the primary source of energy for Earth.
Explain why some cultures have festivals that honor the sun.
Name a festival that celebrates the sun.

content: A bicycle is a two-wheeled vehicle that is powered by a rider's pedaling. It is commonly used for transportation and recreation.
learning_objectives: Identify the main parts of a bicycle
Explain how a bicycle moves forward
List common uses for a bicycle

content: Identifying bell sounds, recognizing the need for wheelchairs, understanding the role of wheels in aiding movement
learning_objectives: Identify the sound of a school bell
Recognize the importance of wheelchairs
Understand the role of wheels in transportation

content: A bicycle has two wheels, a frame, handlebars, a seat, and pedals. The rider sits on the seat and uses the pedals to move the bicycle forward. Bicycles are powered by human energy and do not require fuel like cars.
learning_objectives: Identify the main parts of a bicycle
Explain how a rider makes the bicycle move
Compare the energy source of bicycles to cars

content: In this section, we explore the concept of play and games among children. Play is an essential part of childhood, allowing children to learn and grow. Games are structured forms of play that often involve rules and objectives.
learning_objectives: Children engage in various games during their playtime.
Games can include activities like skipping rope, hide and seek, and bat and ball.
Play and games are important for children's development and enjoyment.

topic: Making a Ball and Playing a Game
content: Babli and Jeet created a ball using rags, paper, wool, and a string to play a game similar to bat and ball.
learning_objectives: Identify the materials used to make the ball
Recognize the game played with the homemade ball
Understand the importance of creativity with available resources

content: Explain clearly in simple language how recycling and repurposing materials can lead to fun and creative activities, teaching children about resourcefulness and sustainability.
learning_objectives: Identify objects around the home that can be repurposed for fun activities
Understand how recycling helps the environment
Think of creative ways to use old items in new ways

content: Suresh went to school. Gopal went to school. I like to eat mangoes. I like to eat grapes. The cat sat on the mat. The dog sat on the mat. This ball has yellow dots on it. This ball also has green dots on it.
learning_objectives: Understand simple sentences
Practice partner dialogue
Identify key ideas

content: Children will learn about the games their parents used to play and communicate the same in English during the next class. They are encouraged to use some of the game-related terms from their mother tongue.
learning_objectives: Identify the games played by parents.
Describe the rules and objectives of the games.
Discuss the cultural significance of these games.

topic: Emulating Various Animals Through Movement and Sound
content: Recognizing and imitating animal sounds, understanding the movements associated with different animals, appreciating the fun in being like various animals
learning_objectives: Recognize and imitate animal sounds
Understand and act out animal movements
Appreciate the fun in being like various animals

content: Animals and birds exhibit a variety of behaviors and possess unique characteristics that allow them to survive and thrive in their environments.
learning_objectives: Understand that animals have evolved to adapt to their specific habitats.
Identify examples of animals that can swim and those that live in trees.
Recognize the importance of understanding animal behaviors and characteristics.

content: Write a few sentences about the picture. Reprint 2026-27
learning_objectives: Understand that animals have different ways of moving
Recognize that each animal's movement is unique to its species
Observe animals to learn about their behavior

content: The ability to understand or perceive something without directly using our eyes, illustrated through the story of Little Onshangla who identifies objects by touch with her mother's help.
learning_objectives: Understand the concept of 'Seeing without Seeing'
Identify how senses can compensate for each other
Recognize examples of using non-visual senses to understand the world

content: Use your senses to identify objects. Smell, touch, and listen to find out what an object is. Taste with caution and only when safe.
learning_objectives: Describe the smell of a rose
Identify the sound a bell makes when shaken
Describe how a glass of water feels when touched

topic: Sight Words and New Vocabulary in Language Development
content: This section explores the importance of sight words and new vocabulary in language development.
learning_objectives: Sight words are essential for reading fluency.
New words help in understanding and expressing complex ideas.
Vocabulary development is crucial for effective communication.

content: Practice using various objects to enhance learning, engage in sensory activities like smelling and tasting to connect with the material, encourage writing and drawing to express understanding and creativity
learning_objectives: Practice with objects
Engage in sensory activities
Encourage writing and drawing

content: Our eyes are vital for our daily activities. It's important to take care of them.
learning_objectives: Protect your eyes from harmful activities.
Maintain good posture while reading or drawing.
Consume a diet rich in vegetables and carrots for eye health.
